docs.title: Portfolio Projects
docs.text: Welcome to my technical portfolio. Below you'll find a collection of projects that demonstrate my skills in API documentation and AI app workfows.

docs.title: File API
docs.text:
A secure REST API built with Python and FastAPI, showcasing API development and documentation best practices.

Key Features: - File download functionality with folder-based organization - File listing endpoints with directory navigation - Enterprise-level security for internet-exposed endpoints - Interactive Swagger UI documentation

Technologies: Python, FastAPI, OpenAPI, Swagger UI

View Project

This portfolio is built with MkDocs and deployed using GitHub Pages. Each project includes detailed documentation, code examples, and deployment information.

Contact: mrjeremiahmichaels@icloud.com | LinkedIn

docs.title: Jeremiah Michaels
docs.text:
I have been a technical writer for over 15 years, focusing on SaaS applications in the Vendor Management and Fintech spaces.

I have extensive experience working alongside developers, product managers, QA, and even sales. My career started in journalism. My time was spent chasing facts, and talking to people to get them. My natural love of technology led me into a techincal writing career. I can learn what I need to, and fill knowledge gaps with interviews and the internet. Then I write the docs.

When I'm not behind a screen, I am a typical Californian, spending as much time as I can outside. I enjoy just about any aspect of nature.

docs.title: Get a File
docs.text:
Retrieves a file by name from a specified folder.

GET /files/{folder}/{filename}

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description Example folder string Yes The folder to look in folder1 filename string Yes The name of the file to retrieve doc1.txt

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text: curl http://localhost:8000/files/folder1/doc1.txt

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: Status Description 200 Returns the file as a binary stream 400 Invalid path 404 File not found

docs.title: Example Error Response
docs.text:
{
  "detail": "File 'doc1.txt' not found in folder 'folder1'."
}

docs.title: Get File Count
docs.text:
Returns the number of files in a specified folder.

GET /files/{folder}/count

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description Example folder string Yes The folder to count files in folder1

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text: curl http://localhost:8000/files/folder1/count

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: Status Description 200 Returns folder name and file count 400 Invalid path 404 Folder not found

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
{
  "folder": "folder1",
  "file_count": 3
}

docs.title: Example Error Response
docs.text:
{
  "detail": "Folder 'folder1' not found."
}

docs.title: File API Overview
docs.text:
The File API is a locally hosted REST API built with Python and FastAPI. It serves files and file metadata from a structured folder directory on the host machine.

This API was built as a portfolio project to demonstrate API development and documentation practices, including AI-assisted development workflows using Claude Desktop.

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text: Environment URL Local http://localhost:8000

docs.title: Endpoints Summary
docs.text: Method Path Description GET /files/{folder}/{filename} Retrieve a file GET /files/{folder}/count Get file count in folder

docs.title: Security
docs.text: This API uses enterprise-level security practices for endpoints exposed to the internet. All requests are validated server-side, and path traversal attacks are mitigated via input validation on the folder and filename parameters.

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
All errors return a JSON object with a detail field describing the issue.

{
  "detail": "File 'doc1.txt' not found in folder 'folder1'."
}

See Endpoints for the full list of error responses per endpoint.

docs.title: Idea
docs.text:
The idea for the file-api is to showcase my API documentation abilities. I used claude desktop to code a locally hosted API that I could generate an OpenAPI spec from and deploy with the Swagger UI.

I wanted a secure location since the API is exposed to the internet.

docs.title: Frameworks
docs.text: I used python and fastapi for the framework. I wanted something that was easy to create and deploy. fastapi create a yml file by default.

docs.title: Prompt
docs.text:
I first prompted claude desktop about what my goal was. I asked what would be the easiest to do, and why. I was presented with three options, but the end of the output suggested that I use the python \ fastapi combo since it accomplished my goals and was the most straightforward.

Next, I outlined two core API functions. The first function downloads a txt file from a specified folder, requiring the folder name as an input parameter. The second function retrieves a list of all files within a given folder, also requiring the folder name as a parameter.

I made sure to add that I wanted the highest level of security since I would be testing the endpoints on my local when it's connected to the internet.

docs.title: Explore the Docs
docs.text: The finished documentation is in the API Docs section, including a full endpoint reference and interactive Swagger UI.
